questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: On the Udden–Wentworth scale, particles between 1/16 mm and 2 mm are classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sand: 1/16–2 mm; silt 1/256–1/16; clay <1/256; gravel >2.

**Working.**
Sand.

**Answer.** Sand

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: A well-sorted, well-rounded quartz arenite is texturally and compositionally
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High sorting/rounding and quartz dominance = mature.

**Working.**
Mature.

**Answer.** Mature

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: In Dunham's classification, a mud-supported limestone with >10% grains is a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dunham: mudstone (<10% grains), wackestone (>10%, mud-supported), packstone, grainstone (grain-supported).

**Working.**
Wackestone.

**Answer.** Wackestone

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: Which primary sedimentary structures indicate current (traction) transport?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cross-beds, ripples and traction lamination are current structures; graded bedding records waning suspension (turbidity) flow.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 4.

**Answer.** Current structures

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: The progressive loss of porosity in a sandstone with burial is mainly due to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mechanical compaction and cementation reduce porosity during diagenesis.

**Working.**
Compaction & cementation.

**Answer.** Compaction and cementation
